functions.comment: Retail buffered-chat reset helper used during CG_Init and again by the clearChat server command; the closest source analogue is CG_InitTeamChat, but retail clears a timed chat ring instead of three flat strings.

functions.signature: static void CG_PushPrintString(const char *text, int type, int holdTime)
functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail name for the timed buffered chat/print writer: it rotates the live entry into the 24-slot history ring when needed, timestamps the new active slot, trims a trailing newline, and copies the new text into the live print record.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawNewChatArea(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail timed chat-stack renderer reached from the ownerdraw dispatcher for the new chat area; HLIL only exposes the scale float directly because the ownerdraw call frame carries the rect/color context.
Retail `tcmd` console-command handler; HLIL resolves the same `gc %i %i` crosshair-target dispatch as the public-source CG_TargetCommand_f.
Retail `sizeup` console-command handler that increments `cg_viewsize` by ten, matching the public-source CG_SizeUp_f.
Retail `sizedown` console-command handler that decrements `cg_viewsize` by ten, matching the public-source CG_SizeDown_f.
Retail `viewpos` console-command handler; HLIL prints the current view origin and yaw with the same format used by CG_Viewpos_f.
Retail `+scores` handler that raises the score-display latch and refreshes the scoreboard when the cached scores are stale, matching CG_ScoresDown_f.
Retail `-scores` handler that clears the score-display latch, matching the public-source CG_ScoresUp_f.

Retail `loadhud` console-command wrapper that resets the menu parser, reads `cg_hudFiles`, falls back to `ui/hud.txt`, and reloads the HUD. This resolves the earlier `0x10006E40` loader-clone ambiguity as CG_LoadHud_f.
Retail `tell_target` wrapper that builds `tell %i %s` for the current crosshair target, matching CG_TellTarget_f.
Retail `tell_attacker` wrapper that targets the last attacker with `tell %i %s`, matching CG_TellAttacker_f.
Retail `vtell_target` wrapper that builds the same `vtell %i %s` dispatch as CG_VoiceTellTarget_f.
Retail `vtell_attacker` wrapper that targets the last attacker with `vtell %i %s`, matching CG_VoiceTellAttacker_f.
Retail `nextTeamMember` command wrapper that advances team follow state, matching the public-source CG_NextTeamMember_f.
Retail `prevTeamMember` command wrapper that reverses team follow state, matching the public-source CG_PrevTeamMember_f.
Retail `nextOrder` handler that cycles the order selector, skips unavailable tasks, and refreshes the order timeout, matching CG_NextOrder_f.
Retail `confirmOrder` handler that sends the confirm voice command and applies the pending team task, matching CG_ConfirmOrder_f.
Retail `denyOrder` handler that sends the deny voice command and clears the pending order state, matching CG_DenyOrder_f.
Retail `taskOffense` wrapper that emits `vsay_team onoffense` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskOffense_f.
Retail `taskDefense` wrapper that emits `vsay_team ondefense` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskDefense_f.
Retail `taskPatrol` wrapper that emits `vsay_team onpatrol` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskPatrol_f.
Retail `taskCamp` wrapper that emits `vsay_team oncamping` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskCamp_f.
Retail `taskFollow` wrapper that emits `vsay_team onfollow` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskFollow_f.
Retail `taskRetrieve` wrapper that emits `vsay_team onreturnflag` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskRetrieve_f.
Retail `taskEscort` wrapper that emits `vsay_team onfollowcarrier` and updates team-task state, matching CG_TaskEscort_f.
Retail `taskOwnFlag` wrapper that announces `ihaveflag`, matching CG_TaskOwnFlag_f.
Retail `tauntKillInsult` wrapper that dispatches `cmd vsay kill_insult`, matching CG_TauntKillInsult_f.
Retail `tauntPraise` wrapper that dispatches `cmd vsay praise`, matching CG_TauntPraise_f.
Retail `tauntTaunt` wrapper that dispatches `cmd vtaunt`, matching CG_TauntTaunt_f.
Retail `tauntDeathInsult` wrapper that dispatches `cmd vsay death_insult`, matching CG_TauntDeathInsult_f.
Retail `tauntGauntlet` wrapper that dispatches `cmd vsay kill_gauntlet`, correcting the old public-source typo while otherwise matching CG_TauntGauntlet_f.
Retail `taskSuicide` wrapper that tells the selected teammate to suicide, matching CG_TaskSuicide_f.
Retail-only `dropflag` command wrapper that gates flag drops by gametype before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `droppowerup` command wrapper that validates team and mode restrictions before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `droprune` command wrapper that enforces the rune-capable mode gate before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `dropweapon` command wrapper that validates the current ruleset before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `readyup` command wrapper that checks the current warmup state before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `team` command wrapper that forwards the requested team string and then refreshes the local team/follow state.
Retail-only `forfeit` command wrapper that enforces the active duel/race conditions before forwarding the server command.
Retail-only `ragequit` command wrapper that forwards the server command and arms the local quit-state side effect observed in HLIL.
Retail-only `setteamcolor` wrapper around the shared color-parse helper at `0x10007980`; it updates the `cg_team*Color` cvars.
Retail-only `setenemycolor` wrapper around the shared color-parse helper at `0x10007980`; it updates the `cg_enemy*Color` cvars.
Retail startOrbit console-command handler; it reads developer and toggles the orbit-camera third-person cvars exactly like the public-source CG_StartOrbit_f.
Retail +chat console-command handler that raises the buffered chat-history latch consumed by the new-chat ownerdraw path.
Retail -chat console-command handler that clears the buffered chat-history latch consumed by the new-chat ownerdraw path.
Retail togglechathistory console-command handler; it flips the same chat-history visibility latch used by +chat and -chat.
Retail print console-command handler that concatenates Argv(1..) and forwards the resulting line into the buffered chat/print ring writer at 0x10006910.
Retail clientmute console-command handler that parses a client slot, reads the backing identity words from the clientinfo block, and forwards them through a private native mute/social import.
Named export that installs the cgame syscall table.

Retail varargs print helper wired into the HUD callback table as cgDC.print; HLIL forwards directly to trap_Print, which matches the narrower Com_Printf role better than source CG_Printf with its chat-beep side path.

functions.signature: void QDECL Com_Error(int level, const char *error, ...)
functions.comment: Retail varargs error adapter assigned into the HUD callback table as cgDC.Error; it preserves the Quake-style error level parameter before forwarding into the cgame error path.
Registers the retail cvar table, pins the model/headmodel defaults, publishes cg_version, and clears ui_voteactive.
Refreshes the registered cvar table and triggers the tracked client-refresh path before frame rendering continues.
Native cgame API-table slot returning the crosshair-traced client index while cg.crosshairClientTime is still live.
Native cgame API-table slot returning the last attacker from the current playerstate while cg.attackerTime is active.
Native cgame API-table slot that reads Argv(0), scans the static console-command table, dispatches the matched handler, and returns qtrue or qfalse.
Retail console-command registrar that walks the same command table as CG_ConsoleCommand and issues trap_AddCommand calls during CG_Init.

Retail rank/place formatter used by obituary and scoreboard text. HLIL preserves the tied-rank mask handling plus the ordinal-suffix selection from the source helper even though the recovered calling convention carries extra register noise.

Retail obituary handler with the target-range guard, local kill-centerprint variants, frozen/thawed obituary text, delayed follow-killer scheduling, and the final obituary print selection from the source CG_Obituary path.
Retail use-item event helper with the invalid-item guard, local centerprint for "No item to use" / "Use %s", medkit usage timestamp update, and the expected holdable-use sound dispatch.

Retail pain-event helper reached from EV_PAIN. HLIL still shows the same 500 ms debounce, painTime update, painDirection toggle, and player pain-sound dispatch that define the source CG_PainEvent contract.

functions.signature: static void CG_TryAutoFollowPowerup(int clientNum, qboolean fromTeamSound)
functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail spectator helper. The only HLIL callsites are the global powerup-pickup path plus GTS_RED_TAKEN / GTS_BLUE_TAKEN; it then applies narrow spectator/follow-mode gates and emits `follow %d%s`, using the ` pw` suffix only on the direct powerup path.

HLIL-only native entry-table wrapper for the retail mouse-input export: it copies the live cursor globals into the cgDC cursor fields and tail-jumps into the shared HUD mouse handler, matching the CG_MOUSE_EVENT path in the source vmMain.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the retail client-identity marshaller: it validates the client slot, copies the sidecar words at 0x10A42400 / 0x10A42418 / 0x10A4241C, and copies two 0x27-byte identity strings into the caller buffer.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the 640-space chat-line Y selector. The host chat-input draw path at 0x004B4100 calls slot data_146cc38 + 0x44 before its SCR_AdjustFrom640-style scaler, and the retail body returns 413 or 455 depending on data_10A9C214.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the 640-space chat-line width selector. The same host draw path calls slot data_146cc38 + 0x48, and the retail body returns 640 or 300 depending on data_10A9C214.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the chat field_t.widthInChars selector. Host say / say_team openers store slot data_146cc38 + 0x4C directly into data_1648C88, which is the widthInChars member of the live field_t rooted at data_1648C80; retail returns 73 or 30 depending on data_10A9C214.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the retail speaking-state setter: it writes the per-client speaking flag at 0x10A4240C, timestamps 0x10A42410 with cg.time, and returns the client block pointer.

functions.signature: void CG_Init(int serverMessageNum, int serverCommandSequence, int clientNum)
functions.comment: HLIL-only function boundary matching the retail startup sequence; this address is not present in the committed Ghidra function inventory.
Native cgame API-table slot that performs the retail teardown path, issues the shutdown-side syscall, and restores ui_mainmenu before returning.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the first tracked-slot notifier: it arms the 3000 ms latch later consumed by CG_SHOW_IF_1ST_PLYR_TRACKED, reloads cg_lastmsg, and replays that text through the buffered print stack.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the second tracked-slot notifier: it arms the 3000 ms latch later consumed by CG_SHOW_IF_2ND_PLYR_TRACKED, reloads cg_lastmsg, and replays that text through the buffered print stack.

Retail particle reset helper identified by the 0x1F000 memset, 0x7C-stride free-list rebuild, and the single explode1 shader-animation registration table rooted at data_10076658 / data_100766d8 / data_10076758.

Retail active-particle update loop reached from the main client frame path; HLIL shows the same lazy init call to CG_ClearParticles, copied view axes, rolling view-basis update, active-list walk, and per-type lifetime handling used by the source particle system.

functions.signature: void CG_ParticleExplosion(const char *animStr, vec3_t origin, vec3_t vel, int duration, int sizeStart, int sizeEnd)
functions.comment: Retail explode1 particle spawner matched by the literal explode1 lookup, the two diagnostic strings, the free-list-to-active-list push, the 0.5 alpha seed, and the 20/30 size progression scaled by the animation aspect ratio table.
Sound and announcer registration pass reached from CG_Init after the retail loading string switches to "sounds".
Graphics and media registration pass reached from CG_Init after the retail loading string switches to "graphics".
Client registration loop reached from CG_Init after the retail loading string switches to "clients".
Tiny configstring accessor with bounds checking against the cached gameState offsets.
Parses the CS_MUSIC configstring and starts the background tracks.
Menu parser with fallback to ui/hud.txt, size guards, Menu_Reset, and loadmenu token handling.

functions.signature: qboolean CG_OwnerDrawHandleKey(int ownerDraw, int flags, float *special, int key)
functions.comment: HUD callback-table stub assigned into cgDC.ownerDrawHandleKey; the retail body is a pure qfalse return.
HUD feeder-count callback that tallies scoreboard and team-list rows across the retail feeder ids.
Retail cgDC.feederItemImage target that dispatches across scoreboard/team feeder variants, unlike the older public-source stub that simply returned 0.

functions.signature: const char *CG_FeederItemText(float feederID, int index, int column, qhandle_t *handle)
functions.comment: HUD feeder-text callback that formats the retail scoreboard and team-list name, score, ping, ready, and spectator columns.
Value-returning cgDC callback that parses the current cvar string through atof, falling back to trap_Cvar_VariableStringBuffer when needed.

functions.signature: void CG_Cvar_GetString(const char *cvar, char *buffer, int bufsize)
functions.comment: Deliberate adapter name for the retail cgDC.getCVarString slot. HLIL first checks the native token helper used by the client and, when that path is empty, falls back to trap_Cvar_VariableStringBuffer exactly like the public callback contract expects.

functions.signature: void CG_Text_PaintWithCursor(float x, float y, float scale, vec4_t color, const char *text, int cursorPos, char cursor, int limit, int style)
functions.comment: Thin HUD bridge wrapper that forwards the cursor-aware text draw call into the shared text painter.
HUD callback that computes text widths for ownerdraw labels such as the gametype string and the obituary-style 'Fragged by %s' line.

functions.signature: int CG_PlayCinematic(const char *name, float x, float y, float w, float h)
functions.comment: HUD cinematic callback wrapping trap_CIN_PlayCinematic with integer extents and the retail looping mode.
HUD cinematic callback tail-jumping directly into trap_CIN_StopCinematic.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawCinematic(int handle, float x, float y, float w, float h)
functions.comment: HLIL-only cgDC draw-cinematic wrapper boundary recovered from the direct slot assignment at data_10a256e0: it converts the float extents, calls trap_CIN_SetExtents, and then calls trap_CIN_DrawCinematic exactly like the public-source CG_DrawCinematic.
HUD cinematic callback tail-jumping directly into trap_CIN_RunCinematic.
Retail helper that reads cg_hudFiles, falls back to ui/hud.txt, calls CG_LoadMenus, and performs the loading half of the source-level HUD bootstrap.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the split cgDC bootstrap helper. The body mirrors the callback assignments that current source still keeps inline in CG_LoadHudMenu and finishes by publishing &cgDC through the ui_shared Init_Display context pointer.
Registers the shared UI art cache used by the cgame HUD/menu code.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawLevelTimer(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_LEVELTIMER` ownerdraw that formats the live clock as `%i:%i%i` before routing through the standard centered text path.
HLIL-only callback-table target that returns ownerdraw-driven score, stat, and selected-player values for the HUD scripting layer.
Retail HUD visibility predicate assigned into cgDC.ownerDrawVisible; evaluates ownerdraw bitmasks against gametype, team, follow, vote, and scoreboard state.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawKiller(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, qhandle_t shader, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail HUD obituary-line helper that centers the local killer text. HLIL uses cg.killerName directly and inlines the CG_GetKillerText formatting step before measuring and drawing the "Fragged by %s" string.
Small scoreboard-timer helper that reads the retail match clock in milliseconds, clamps negative values to zero, and rounds to whole seconds with the same +500 bias as the source helper.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawCapFragLimit(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, qhandle_t shader, int textStyle)
functions.comment: HUD score-limit drawer that selects frag, capture, or score limits from the same gametype buckets as the source helper and paints the chosen numeric limit through the shared text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawClanArenaPlayers(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle, team_t team)
functions.comment: Retail ownerdraw helper reached from `CG_RED_CLAN_PLYRS` and `CG_BLUE_CLAN_PLYRS`; HLIL selects the round-based per-team count and paints it through the shared numeric text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawPlayerCount(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle, qboolean friendly)
functions.comment: Retail ownerdraw helper reached from `CG_TEAM_PLYR_COUNT` and `CG_ENEMY_PLYR_COUNT`; HLIL swaps the local team when the enemy slot is requested and paints the centered living-player count through the same numeric path as the source helper.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawRoundLabel(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail round-label ownerdraw that emits either "Warmup" or "Round %d" before routing through the shared text alignment path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawDominationOwnedFlags(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle, team_t team)
functions.comment: Retail ownerdraw helper reached from `CG_RED_OWNED_FLAGS` and `CG_BLUE_OWNED_FLAGS`; HLIL selects the per-team Domination owned-flag counter and paints it through the shared numeric HUD text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawGameLimit(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail legacy `CG_GAME_LIMIT` ownerdraw that selects one of the exact `Cap Limit`, `Frag Limit`, `Round Limit`, or `Score Limit` formats and centers the result. Narrower than the current source helper, but clearly the same HUD slot.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawStartingWeapons(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_STARTING_WEAPONS` ownerdraw that walks the starting-weapon mask and renders the configured loadout through the intro-panel draw path. The current source helper prints a text list instead, but the owning slot and role still line up cleanly.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawLocalTime(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_LOCALTIME` ownerdraw. Unlike the current source helper's `HH:MM` output, retail formats a full local time/date label with month, day, and year before routing through the shared text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawMatchEndCondition(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_MATCH_END_CONDITION` ownerdraw that selects the winning condition text family for race, team, round-limit, mercy, score-limit, and end-of-game cases before drawing it directly.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawMapName(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_MAP_NAME` ownerdraw that paints the prebuilt current-map label buffer directly through the HUD text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawMatchDetails(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_MATCH_DETAILS` ownerdraw that formats the current phase label plus gametype and the prebuilt panel string via `%s - %s - %s`. Narrower than the newer source helper, but clearly the same overlay slot.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawGameTypeMap(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_GAME_TYPE_MAP` ownerdraw that formats the gametype label plus the same prebuilt panel string through `%s - %s`. The current source helper now expands that slot to a three-part location/map line.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawSelectedPlayerAccuracy(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail selected-player accuracy ownerdraw for `CG_SELECTED_PLYR_ACCURACY`; HLIL formats the tracked scoreboard client's accuracy as `%d%%` and paints it through the standard text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawSelectedPlayerBestWeapon(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail selected-player best-weapon ownerdraw for `CG_PLYR_BEST_WEAPON_NAME`; HLIL maps the tracked weapon index to the same human-readable weapon-name family used by the source helper.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawEndGameScore(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail `CG_PLYR_END_GAME_SCORE` ownerdraw that emits the richer local endgame summary family for placement, score, assists, defends, captures, skulls, and forfeits before painting the selected text line.
Retail HUD icon drawer that selects the pre-registered gametype art handle for the current gametype and paints it into the supplied rect.

Retail endgame ownerdraw reached from `CG_1STPLACE_PLYR_MODEL`; HLIL forwards the first-place client slot through the shared profile/model draw helper and matches the non-active scoreboard role of the source analogue.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the old `CG_PLAYERMODEL` ownerdraw wrapper. HLIL reaches it from ownerdraw case `0x1A`, selects either the tracked client slot or the live local client slot, and forwards that client through the same shared profile/model draw helper used by `CG_DrawFirstPlaceModel`.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the match-status text compositor consumed by the HUD draw and width path. HLIL prefixes the current match-phase label and appends the same placement or team-score text family exposed by CG_GetGameStatusText.
Retail status-text helper used by both ownerdraw width and the match-status HUD path; HLIL preserves the free-for-all placement string and the red/blue team-score lead messages.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawGameStatus(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, qhandle_t shader, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Thin HUD text wrapper that draws the retail CG_GetGameStatusText result at the ownerdraw baseline.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawGameType(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, qhandle_t shader, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail gametype-text ownerdraw that selects the current gametype label from the same static gametype string table used by CG_OwnerDrawWidth and paints it through the shared text path.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawMatchStatus(rectDef_t *rect, float text_x, float text_y, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail match-status ownerdraw. Instead of composing the label inline like the current source tree, it pulls the already-combined retail status string from the synthetic helper at 0x10034A00 and then draws it with the standard HUD alignment rules.

functions.signature: static void CG_DrawSpectatorMessages(rectDef_t *rect, float scale, vec4_t color, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Retail spectator-message ownerdraw reached from `CG_SPEC_MESSAGES`; HLIL draws the older `Round In Progress`, `SPECTATOR MODE`, `waiting to play`, and mouse-cycle hint text family in the same HUD slot used by the source helper.

functions.signature: void CG_OwnerDraw(float x, float y, float w, float h, float text_x, float text_y, int ownerDraw, int ownerDrawFlags, int align, float special, float scale, vec4_t color, qhandle_t shader, int textStyle)
functions.comment: Large ownerdraw dispatcher assigned into cgDC.ownerDrawItem; switches over the HUD ownerdraw IDs and forwards to the expected draw helpers.
Native cgame API-table slot that updates the retail HUD interaction mode; the retail event ids extend or shift the public-source enum, but the state-setting behavior still matches CG_EventHandling.

Retail key-input handler reached from the native export table; unlike the GPL path, it inspects the bound command string directly to intercept messagemode, screenshot, +voice, and +scores behavior for the spectator/HUD overlay.
HLIL-only menu-script bridge assigned into cgDC.runScript; parses HUD script tokens and handles the retail fullscreen toggle commands before issuing vid_restart.
HLIL-only callback-table target that writes the red, blue, or neutral HUD tint vector based on the local player's team.
Deferred player-model loader that walks the client list, skips low-memory cases, and promotes deferred registrations exactly like the public-source CG_LoadDeferredPlayers.
Large event dispatcher handling footsteps, item pickup, weapon fire, obituary flow, and the unknown-event fatal path.
Prediction loop with prediction error, dropped-event, and double-event debug output.
Server-command configstring update dispatcher that reads Argv(1), parses the changed index, and switches over special handling paths.
Map restart reset path that clears transient state, restarts music, and resets vote and third-person state.
Small server-command helper that issues the retail stoprecord console command variant with an added wait.
Server-command helper that forwards record requests to the console command buffer, preserving the optional demo name.
Server-command helper that forwards screenshot requests to screenshotJPEG, preserving the optional filename.
Loads a voice-chat script, enforces the MAX_VOICEFILESIZE guard, parses the list header, and returns the cached voice-chat index or -1.

functions.signature: qboolean CG_GetVoiceChat(voiceChatList_t *voiceChatList, const char *id, sfxHandle_t *snd, char **chat)
functions.comment: Looks up a voice-chat id inside a loaded list and picks a random sound/chat pair from the matching entry.
Builds the head-model lookup path, consults the per-headmodel cache, tries scripts/%s.vc, and falls back by gender when no exact voice-chat file is available.
Plays the buffered voice chat, opens the voice menu for order prompts, updates accept-order state, and echoes text chat when allowed.
Drains the buffered voice-chat ring once cg.voiceChatTime expires, plays the oldest entry, and delays the next playback by one second.
Copies a voice chat into the ring buffer and forces playback of the oldest entry when the buffer wraps.

functions.signature: void CG_VoiceChatLocal(int mode, qboolean voiceOnly, int clientNum, int color, const char *cmd)
functions.comment: Normalizes the client index, resolves the client's voice-chat list, formats the colored chat line, and queues the buffered local voice chat.
Tiny in-place filter that strips the 0x19 chat-escape byte before text is printed or added to team chat.
Main cgame server-command dispatcher covering configstring updates, race commands, vote UI control, map_restart, and other client-side commands.
Snapshot handoff routine that advances cg.snap to cg.nextSnap and fires entity transition events.
Snapshot pump that reads the next snapshot, enforces server-time ordering, and calls CG_TransitionSnapshot when needed.
Retail `+zoom` command handler that arms zoom state and preserves the same timing and toggle semantics as CG_ZoomDown_f.
Retail `-zoom` command handler that releases zoom state and preserves the same timing and toggle semantics as CG_ZoomUp_f.

Deliberate synthetic native-export name for the one-word time getter. The host uses slot data_146cc38 + 0x38 when formatting [m:ss.mmm] notify/chat prefixes, falling back to host serverTime; the retail body itself returns cg.physicsTime.

functions.signature: void CG_DrawActiveFrame(int serverTime, stereoFrame_t stereoView, qboolean demoPlayback)
functions.comment: Top-level render/update frame loop that drives snapshot processing, prediction, scene build, and HUD presentation.
Retail `weapnext` command handler for cycling the selected weapon, matching CG_NextWeapon_f.
Retail `weapprev` command handler for cycling the selected weapon backward, matching CG_PrevWeapon_f.
Retail `weapon` command handler that keeps the public-source selection path and also accepts the retail `toggle` argument variant.
